what_it_does: Stores a machine-readable graph of the project's files and folders: each node carries identity, path, extension, role, size, and symbol-level defines/uses arrays; edges record parent-child containment relationships.
observations: Contains a large `nodes` array (600+ entries) covering docs, code, CSS, HTML cards, images, archives, ingest assets, QA screenshots, and UI kits.
Each node uses a flat schema: `id`, `name`, `path`, `parent`, `type`, `ext`, `role`, `size`, `defines`, `uses`.
`role` values form a project-specific taxonomy: doc, card, token, app, atomicity, engine, component, ui-kit, ingest, shot, specimen, template, axis, etc.
`defines` and `uses` hold `CV_*` symbol identifiers (e.g., `CV_AI`, `CV_REGISTRY`, `CV_GLYPHIC`) for cross-reference, even though visible edges are mostly `contains`.
Edges are simple `contains` links from parent folder id to child file/folder id.
Image and screenshot nodes often have `size: null` while text/code nodes carry byte sizes.
Root-level files like `README.md`, `DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md`, and `SKILL.md` are present alongside design-token CSS files.
concerns: The visible edge set is almost exclusively `contains`; the richer `defines`/`uses` symbol relationships are declared on nodes but not yet materialized as edges in the shown portion.
No timestamp or generator version is recorded, so freshness and provenance of the map are not file-local.
Several folder nodes appear after their children in the array, which is valid JSON but makes human scanning slightly harder.
notes: This is the project's own index of itself: a neutral ledger that tries to catalog every substrate file and its symbolic exports/imports.
questions: Are there additional edge kinds beyond `contains` later in the file, or is the graph intentionally containment-only?
Is this file hand-maintained or produced by `system/build-system-map.js`?
How are `CV_*` symbols resolved when the same symbol is defined in multiple files?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as a system map/ledger; it does catalog files and symbol references, but the visible graph is mostly a containment tree rather than a full reference graph.
apparent_themes: Graph-based inventory of design and code substrates
Neutral, non-hierarchical documentation of project scope
intention_signals: The file appears designed to make a large, multi-asset design system queryable and navigable by treating every file and exported symbol as a node in a unified graph.
novelty: It merges traditional source files, design assets, screenshots, pitch-deck images, and UI-kit components into one flat graph with a shared role taxonomy and symbol-level annotations.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable inventory that a fused system can use to locate files by role, trace symbol definitions and usages, and build navigational or visualization tools over the whole project.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON system map listing hundreds of project files and folders as graph nodes with roles, sizes, and symbol defines/uses, linked by containment edges.
intention_for_embedding: To create a queryable ledger of all code, design, and documentation substrates in the project.
